sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Trailblazer Merger Corp I appoints new CEO following leadership change
feed.summary: Trailblazer Merger Corp I (NASDAQ: TBMC) has announced that Arie Rabinowitz resigned as CEO and director, with Yosef Eichorn appointed as the new Chief Executive Officer for both the company and Trailblazer Holdings, Inc. Eichorn, who is Rabinowitz's son-in-law and currently serves as Chief Development Officer, will lead the company through its proposed business combination with Cyabra Strategy Ltd., an Israeli private company, which remains subject to shareholder approval. The company has also made recent financial and operational adjustments, including increasing its borrowing limit and extending its business combination deadline.

feed.title: Urban Edge Properties secures $950 million in unsecured credit facilities
feed.summary: Urban Edge Properties has secured $950 million in unsecured credit facilities, extending maturities and increasing its borrowing capacity by $150 million. The new facilities include a $700 million revolving credit facility and $250 million in delayed-draw term loan facilities, enhancing the REIT's financial flexibility. This follows the company's strong third-quarter earnings for 2025, which surpassed analyst expectations in both EPS and revenue.

feed.title: South Plains Financial increases quarterly dividend by 6%
feed.summary: South Plains Financial, Inc. announced a 6% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.17 per share, marking its seventh consecutive year of dividend raises. The bank holding company, trading near its 52-week high, also recently reported strong Q3 2025 earnings and is set to acquire BOH Holdings, Inc. in a $105.9 million deal.

feed.title: Crinetics begins phase 2/3 trial of oral CAH treatment in children
feed.summary: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals has initiated a Phase 2/3 trial (BALANCE-CAH) for atumelnant, an oral treatment for congenital adrenal hyperplasia (CAH) in children, marking a significant step in addressing an unmet medical need. The company's shares are performing well, and analysts are optimistic about its prospects, reinforced by strong financial health and recent positive developments for its other product, Palsonify. Atumelnant, designated as an Orphan Drug, is the first and only small molecule ACTH receptor antagonist in late-stage clinical development for CAH.

feed.title: New TBMC (NASDAQ: TBMC) CEO installed as Cyabra merger moves toward shareholder vote
feed.summary: Trailblazer Merger Corporation I (TBMC) announced a leadership change as its merger with Cyabra Strategy Ltd. proceeds. Arie Rabinowitz resigned as CEO and director, with Yosef Eichorn appointed as the new CEO. The company reassured that Rabinowitz's resignation was not due to disagreements and highlighted Eichorn's background and his family relationship to Rabinowitz.

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies Inc (NYSE:LHX) Plans Quarterly Dividend of $1.25
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies Inc (NYSE:LHX) announced a quarterly dividend of $1.25 per share, representing a 4.2% increase from its previous dividend. The company maintains a strong dividend coverage with a payout ratio of 39.4% and has consistently increased its dividend for 24 consecutive years. Analysts expect the company to continue covering its dividend with a future payout ratio of 38.3%.

feed.title: Honeywell poaches Deere CFO for aerospace spinoff
feed.summary: Deere & Co. CFO Josh Jepsen is leaving to become the finance chief of Honeywell International's aerospace spinoff, Honeywell Aerospace, set to become an independent public company headquartered in Phoenix, Arizona, in the second half of the year. Jepsen's departure from Deere comes as the company faces margin pressures from tariffs, reporting a significant drop in net income for the fiscal year ended November 2025. Honeywell Aerospace has also named three other veterans to its leadership team to ensure long-term success.

feed.title: IBM stock slides in afternoon trade as investors brace for earnings and fresh security news
feed.summary: IBM shares dipped Friday afternoon ahead of its Q4 2025 earnings call next week, with investors focusing on software growth and 2026 guidance. The company also announced a new "quantum-safe" security partnership with Keyfactor, while analysts from Jefferies and Evercore reaffirmed their positive outlooks for IBM. Despite a recent acquisition announcement, the stock's future hinges on its upcoming earnings report and growth projections.

feed.title: Vistra to Report Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results on Feb. 26
feed.summary: Vistra (NYSE: VST) announced it will report its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial and operating results on Thursday, February 26, 2026. The company will host a live conference call and webcast at 10 a.m. ET (9 a.m. CT) accessible via its website. A replay of the webcast will also be available for one year following the call.

feed.title: The Truth About Hologic Inc: Why This ‘Boring’ Stock Could Be a Silent Game-Changer
feed.summary: Hologic Inc (HOLX) is presented as a potentially underestimated investment in women's health, diagnostics, and medical imaging, contrasting with the current hype around AI, EV, and crypto stocks. The article explores Hologic's business model, stock performance, and market position, highlighting its focus on essential healthcare services which could provide stable, long-term growth. It suggests HOLX is a "quiet compounder" more suited for serious, long-term investors than those seeking instant, high-volatility gains.

feed.title: Essential Utilities (WTRG) advances American Water merger with IMO launch and Feb. 10 shareholder vote
feed.summary: Essential Utilities (WTRG) has established an Integration Management Office (IMO) with American Water as part of their planned merger, focusing on culture, governance, organization, and technology. The merger is expected to close in Q1 2027, and a virtual shareholder vote for approval is scheduled for February 10, 2026. The company also provided extensive cautionary language regarding forward-looking statements and directed investors to SEC filings for detailed information.

feed.title: NRG Energy raises quarterly dividend by 8% to $0.475/share
feed.summary: NRG Energy (NYSE:NRG) has announced an 8% increase in its quarterly dividend, raising it to $0.475 per share, up from the previous $0.44 per share. The new dividend is payable on February 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 1, 2026. This adjustment brings the company's annualized dividend to $1.90 per share, reflecting a forward yield of 3.86%.

feed.title: Enphase Energy (NASDAQ: ENPH) plans layoffs and targets lower operating expenses in 2026
feed.summary: Enphase Energy (NASDAQ: ENPH) has announced a restructuring plan to align its workforce and costs with business needs, targeting lower operating expenses by 2026. This plan includes reducing its staff by less than 6% (approximately 160 employees), shifting some functions to lower-cost regions, and focusing on distribution-led sales, core R&D, and increased use of AI and automation. The company anticipates incurring about $4.6 million in restructuring and asset impairment charges, aiming to reduce non-GAAP operating expenses to $70–$75 million per quarter starting in Q3 2026.

feed.title: Essential Declares March 2026 Dividend
feed.summary: Essential Utilities Inc. has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.3426 per share, payable on March 2, 2026, to shareholders of record on February 9, 2026. This marks 80 years of consecutive quarterly cash dividends and 35 dividend increases in the last 34 years for the company. Essential Utilities focuses on providing water, wastewater, and natural gas services to approximately 5.5 million people across nine states.

feed.title: International Paper’s fluff pulp arm becomes $2.3B stand-alone company
feed.summary: American Industrial Partners has completed its acquisition of International Paper's Global Cellulose Fibers (GCF) business, establishing it as an independent company. GCF, headquartered in Memphis, Tennessee, is now a leading global producer of absorbent fluff pulp, reporting approximately $2.3 billion in revenue for 2024 with 3,300 employees and nine manufacturing facilities worldwide. This divestiture continues International Paper's strategic portfolio reshaping, following its acquisition of DS Smith and other asset sales.

feed.title: U.S. Markets Closed Mixed Friday As Booz Allen Hamilton Led, Intel Lagged
feed.summary: U.S. stock markets closed mixed on Friday, with Booz Allen Hamilton leading and Intel lagging. While the Nasdaq gained 0.28% and the S&P 500 added 0.03%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average declined by 0.58%.

feed.title: Baillie Gifford Q4 2025 Investment Adjustments: Axon Stake Increased by 1198.7%
feed.summary: Baillie Gifford made significant portfolio adjustments in Q4 2025, including adding 21 new stocks, with Medline Inc being the largest at $437.24 million. The firm drastically increased its stake in Axon Enterprise Inc by 1198.7% and fully exited 20 holdings, such as Pinterest Inc and Chewy Inc. Baillie Gifford also reduced positions in 160 stocks, including Cloudflare Inc and Datadog Inc, indicating strategic shifts and risk management.

feed.title: Franklin Templeton Canada Announces ETF Cash Distributions
feed.summary: Franklin Templeton Canada has announced cash distributions for several of its ETFs and ETF series of mutual funds for Canadian investors. Unitholders of record as of January 30, 2026, will receive the specified per-unit cash distribution on February 9, 2026. The announcement highlights Franklin Templeton's diverse ETF platform, offering active, smart beta, and passive ETFs across various asset classes.

feed.title: International Paper Completes Sale of Global Cellulose Fibers Business to American Industrial Partners (AIP)
feed.summary: International Paper (NYSE: IP; LSE: IPC) has finalized the sale of its Global Cellulose Fibers (GCF) business to American Industrial Partners (AIP) for $1.5 billion, including $190 million in preferred stock issued to International Paper. The GCF business, which had a revenue of approximately $2.3 billion in 2024 (excluding closed mills), produces pulp for various applications like personal care products, construction materials, and paints. This transaction affects 3,300 employees globally across nine manufacturing facilities and eight regional offices.

feed.title: 3,300 workers change hands in $1.5B pulp deal at International Paper
feed.summary: International Paper (NYSE: IP) has completed the sale of its Global Cellulose Fibers (GCF) business to funds affiliated with American Industrial Partners (AIP) for $1.5 billion, plus $190 million in preferred stock. This transaction involves approximately 3,300 employees, nine manufacturing facilities, and eight regional offices. The divested GCF segment generated about $2.3 billion in revenue in 2024 (excluding closed mills), marking a significant portfolio adjustment for International Paper.

feed.title: How Federal Realty's 2025 $4.43 payout is taxed for shareholders
feed.summary: Federal Realty Investment Trust (NYSE: FRT) has released the 2025 federal income tax treatment for its distributions to common shares and 5.000% Series C preferred depositary shares. For common shares, a total of $4.43 per share was paid, consisting of taxable ordinary dividends and capital gains, including a portion reported as Sec. 897 capital gain. Series C depositary shares received $1.25 total, also broken down into taxable ordinary dividends and capital gains.

feed.title: Federal Realty Investment Trust Releases Tax Status of 2025 Distributions
feed.summary: Federal Realty Investment Trust (NYSE: FRT) has released the federal income tax treatment for its 2025 distributions to holders of Common Shares of Beneficial Interest and 5.000% Series C Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Shares. The release includes detailed tables outlining record dates, payable dates, distribution amounts per share, and the tax classifications, such as taxable ordinary dividends, capital gains, and nontaxable distributions, for both common and preferred shares. Shareholders are advised to consult their personal tax advisors for specific treatment of these distributions.

feed.title: Lululemon Founder Outraged Over See-Through Leggings: 'A New Low'
feed.summary: Lululemon Athletica founder Chip Wilson has criticized the company's board over quality control issues, calling recent see-through leggings and declining product standards a "new low." Wilson, who remains the largest individual shareholder, attributes these problems to corporate bureaucracy and a diluted brand identity, leading him to launch a proxy contest to replace board members. His actions come as Lululemon faces increased competition and a shifting retail landscape.

feed.title: P/E Ratio Insights for KKR
feed.summary: This article analyzes KKR Inc.'s (NYSE: KKR) current P/E ratio of 51.88, noting it is lower than the Capital Markets industry average of 60.89. It discusses the implications of a lower P/E ratio, suggesting potential undervaluation or weaker growth prospects, and advises investors to consider the P/E ratio alongside other financial metrics and qualitative factors for comprehensive decision-making. The stock currently trades at $124.00, having decreased by 1.81% in the current session.

feed.title: Autodesk to cut 1,000 workers as the tech company bets on AI
feed.summary: Software company Autodesk plans to lay off approximately 1,000 employees, about 7% of its global workforce, as part of a strategic shift towards expanding its leadership in artificial intelligence. While the company stated the cuts are not driven by AI displacing workers, roles in customer-facing sales will be significantly affected, with cost savings reinvested into priorities through fiscal year 2027. This follows previous layoffs in 2025 and positions Autodesk as the latest California tech firm to announce significant job reductions despite an 18% increase in Q3 revenue.

feed.title: Autodesk to cut 1,000 workers as the tech company bets on AI
feed.summary: Autodesk plans to cut approximately 1,000 jobs, or 7% of its global workforce, as part of a "strategic shift" with a focus on expanding its leadership in artificial intelligence. The layoffs will significantly impact customer-facing sales roles, with cost savings reinvested into key priorities. Despite the cuts, Autodesk's CEO clarified that AI is not displacing workers and the company aims to maintain its human-centric approach to technology.

feed.title: Polestar Automotive stock hits 52-week low at 2.33 USD
feed.summary: Polestar Automotive's stock has fallen to a 52-week low of $2.33, marking a significant decline of over 54% in the past year due to challenging market conditions and substantial financial pressure, including a negative gross profit margin and over $5.6 billion in debt. Despite analyst projections for 47% revenue growth, the company faces high volatility and a Nasdaq deficiency notice for its share price, along with a planned change in its ADS ratio.

feed.title: Fire retardant maker completes $685M acquisition of medical device company
feed.summary: Perimeter Solutions (NYSE: PRM), a Clayton-based company known for manufacturing firefighting products, has successfully acquired a medical device business for $685 million. This acquisition marks a significant expansion for Perimeter Solutions into the medical device sector.

feed.title: AGNC Investment Approaches Q4 Earnings Release Amid Market Scrutiny
feed.summary: AGNC Investment Corp. is approaching its Q4 2025 earnings release, which is expected to be critically evaluated by the market given the stock's recent rally and mixed analyst sentiment. While the company's stock is near its annual peak, driven by factors like increased stake from Simplicity Wealth LLC, analysts have reiterated "Hold" ratings with price targets below the current trading level. Investors are particularly focused on the mortgage spread dynamics and how they will impact the company's book value, earnings, and substantial dividend payout.

feed.title: Bank First Corporation Q4 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: Bank First Corporation reported strong financial results for Q4 and full year 2025, with significant growth in net income and EPS. The company's acquisition of Centre 1 Bancorp, Inc. on January 1, 2026, marks the largest in its history, expanding its market presence and service offerings. Management expressed satisfaction with the performance and is optimistic about future growth through integration and market expansion.

feed.title: RTX joins Amazon, Home Depot winning 401(k) forfeiture lawsuit
feed.summary: A federal judge has dismissed a lawsuit against RTX and its $58.65 billion 401(k) plan. Former employees alleged that the use of forfeited company match funds violated federal law and the terms of the plan. RTX joins other companies like Amazon and Home Depot in winning similar 401(k) forfeiture lawsuits.

feed.title: AI set to boost cloud spending — again: S&P Global
feed.summary: Hyperscalers are projected to increase capital spending by nearly 40% in 2026, reaching almost $600 billion, to build out AI infrastructure according to S&P Global. This substantial investment comes as enterprises wait for their own AI investments to yield significant returns, with nearly eight in 10 executives not expecting substantial revenue contributions from AI until 2030. Despite the slow return, enterprise IT budgets are expected to continue increasing their AI investments, driving global IT spend past $6 trillion in 2026.

feed.title: W.W. Grainger's Biggest Threat Is High Expectations (NYSE:GWW)
feed.summary: W.W. Grainger (GWW) continues to demonstrate strong operating performance and gain market share in the U.S. MRO sector, bolstered by its e-commerce platforms Zoro and MonotaRO. The main concern for investors is the company's valuation, as shares are only modestly undervalued with limited catalysts for a significant near-term re-rating. While long-term prospects include outperforming industrial production and strong free cash flow margins, increased competition and pricing transparency are expected to exert pressure on reported margins.

feed.title: Kenvue (KVUE) stock price barely budges as Kimberly-Clark deal vote nears
feed.summary: Kenvue (KVUE) shares saw little movement as investors anticipate a Jan. 29 vote on the company's proposed sale to Kimberly-Clark. Despite the implied valuation of the deal being above current trading prices, analysts point to "deal dynamics" and Tylenol litigation concerns causing the stock to be range-bound. Institutional Shareholder Services has recommended approval, but the litigation overhang remains a key concern for skeptics.

feed.title: A Look At Southern Company’s Valuation After FORTUNE Names It Top Utility For 2024
feed.summary: Southern Company (SO) has been recognized by FORTUNE as the No. 1 utility for 2024, yet its stock price has been relatively stable recently despite strong long-term returns. Analysts suggest the stock is undervalued, with a fair value of $99.23 compared to its current $87.51, driven by significant infrastructure investments and increased electrification demand. However, potential pressure on earnings per share from high capital plans and regulatory approvals could challenge this undervaluation thesis.

feed.title: Should Praxis Precision Medicines’ (PRAX) New Clinical Strategy Chief Reshape Its Long-Term Epilepsy Investment Story?
feed.summary: Praxis Precision Medicines recently appointed Dr. Orrin Devinsky, a renowned neurologist and epilepsy expert, as its new Head of Clinical Strategy. This appointment is expected to significantly influence the quality and credibility of Praxis's clinical packages, particularly in epilepsy, as its late-stage pipeline moves towards commercialization. While the company faces challenges with current sales and losses, this strategic hire could bolster investor confidence in its long-term potential, especially given the wide range of current fair value estimates.

feed.title: Larry Biscotti Joins Sensus Healthcare’s Board of Directors
feed.summary: Sensus Healthcare Inc. has appointed Larry Biscotti to its Board of Directors, increasing the board to six directors, with four being independent. Biscotti brings over 25 years of executive leadership in medical device and healthcare technology, including roles at GE HealthCare, Elekta, and Varian Medical Systems. His expertise in commercial strategy, global market expansion, and oncology technologies is expected to support Sensus Healthcare's mission to advance non-invasive treatments for skin cancer and keloids.

feed.title: Dividends Declared: Energy Majors SLB, ONEOK Lift Payouts as 4Q Reporting Begins
feed.summary: Energy majors SLB and ONEOK have announced increased dividend payouts as the fourth-quarter reporting season commences. This article provides a roundup of dividend declarations from select upstream, midstream, and service and supply sectors for the week of January 19, 2026.

feed.title: NRG Energy Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.475 Per Share, 8% Year-Over-Year Increase
feed.summary: NRG Energy has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.475 per share, an 8% increase from the previous year, demonstrating its commitment to shareholder returns and aligning with its annual growth target of 7-9%. The dividend will be paid on February 17, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 2, 2026. The company continues to focus on innovative natural gas and electricity solutions to drive growth in the North American market, while analysts project a target price increase for NRG stock over the next 12 months.

feed.title: FirstEnergy Crews Ready for Winter Storm; Offer Tips to Stay Safe and Warm During Extreme Cold
feed.summary: FirstEnergy Corp. is preparing for a winter storm and extreme cold across its service area, with crews ready to respond to potential power outages. The company emphasizes its year-round preparation efforts, including tree trimming and equipment upgrades, and provides customers with tips on managing energy use and staying safe during the cold snap. These tips include advice on using space heaters safely, conserving energy, and how to report outages and receive updates.

feed.title: B. Riley Securities Initiates Coverage of Bandwidth (BAND) with Buy Recommendation
feed.summary: B. Riley Securities has initiated coverage of Bandwidth (NASDAQ:BAND) with a Buy recommendation. The average one-year price target for Bandwidth is $24.14/share, representing a 75.05% upside from its latest closing price. Institutional ownership data indicates a slightly decreased number of shares held by institutions, but a bullish put/call ratio of 0.18.

feed.title: Traders Buy High Volume of Lam Research Put Options (NASDAQ:LRCX)
feed.summary: Lam Research Corporation (NASDAQ:LRCX) experienced unusually high put option trading volume on Friday, with 87,781 put options bought, representing a 129% increase from the daily average. This activity occurs amidst a backdrop of mixed analyst ratings and significant insider stock sales by the CFO and a director. Despite some neutral sentiments from analysts regarding valuation and relative underperformance, several analysts have raised price targets citing AI-chip demand and strong growth prospects.

feed.title: eBay bans AI agents from shopping, but hints at future approved bots
feed.summary: eBay is updating its user agreement to explicitly ban AI agents and chatbots from making purchases on its platform, effective February 20, 2026. This move aims to prevent "agentic shopping" by bots competing with human users, although eBay hints it may permit authorized AI agents in the future. The e-commerce giant is also experimenting with its own agentic shopping experiences amidst broader industry trends of AI integration in retail.

feed.title: Western Digital Stock Rose 7% Last Week. Here’s Why $238 Could Be an Important Level
feed.summary: Western Digital stock increased by about 7% last week before pulling back to $238, influenced by optimism around data storage demand and subsequent profit-taking after analyst target updates. Despite the recent dip, TIKR's valuation model suggests the stock is undervalued with a target price of $317, indicating a 30.4% upside over 2.4 years due to sustained demand for high-capacity storage, AI workloads, and improved operating margins.

feed.title: Marsh & McLennan Companies (MRSH) Shares Cross 2% Yield Mark
feed.summary: Marsh & McLennan Companies (MRSH) shares recently broke above the 2% yield mark, offering an annualized dividend of $3.6. This article highlights the importance of dividends for total stock market returns, indicating that a sustainable yield over 2% is particularly attractive. It also notes that MRSH is an S&P 500 company, suggesting stability.

feed.title: 12 ways to stay warm and cut power use in extreme cold, from FirstEnergy crews
feed.summary: FirstEnergy (NYSE: FE) has activated its incident command structure and staged crews to prepare for a winter storm bringing snow and extreme cold to its six-state service area. The company, through its $28 billion Energize365 program, is upgrading equipment and offers customers 12 energy-saving and safety tips, alongside guidance on reporting outages and accessing bill assistance. This proactive communication comes as FirstEnergy manages regulatory settlements and invests billions in grid resilience and infrastructure.

feed.title: Raymond James raises South State stock price target to $120 on strong Q4
feed.summary: Raymond James has increased its price target for South State (NYSE:SSB) to $120 from $115, maintaining a "Strong Buy" rating after the bank's strong fourth-quarter 2025 results. The regional bank surpassed revenue and core EPS estimates, driven by 8% annualized loan and deposit growth, strong fee income, and significant share repurchases. Analysts have revised earnings estimates upwards, and the company is expected to remain profitable, having raised its dividend for 14 consecutive years.

feed.title: NRG Energy Raises Quarterly Dividend to $0.475 a Share From $0.44, Payable Feb. 17 to Holders of Record Feb. 2
feed.summary: NRG Energy (NYSE: NRG) has increased its quarterly dividend to $0.475 per share, up from the previous $0.44 per share. This dividend is scheduled for payment on February 17, 2026, to shareholders who are on record as of February 2, 2026. This announcement highlights NRG Energy's commitment to returning value to its shareholders through increased distributions.

feed.title: Bandwidth (NASDAQ:BAND) Shares Surge 6.5% After B. Riley Initiates Buy Rating with $20 Target
feed.summary: Bandwidth (NASDAQ:BAND) shares surged 6.5% after B. Riley Securities initiated coverage with a 'Buy' rating and a $20 price target, citing the company's unique nationwide all-IP network and strong position to benefit from AI voice applications. Despite this positive development, the stock remains 25.7% below its 52-week high, and the company has experienced significant market volatility. Analysts have a diverse range of price targets for Bandwidth, with an average 1-year target of $24.

feed.title: FirstEnergy Crews Ready for Winter Storm; Offer Tips to Stay Safe and Warm During Extreme Cold
feed.summary: FirstEnergy Corp. is preparing for a winter storm and extreme cold across its service area, with crews ready to respond to potential power outages. The company highlights its year-round preparation efforts, including tree trimming and equipment upgrades, and provides customers with 12 tips for staying safe, managing energy consumption, and reporting outages effectively during the cold weather.
